subject: Re: deselected frames still contributing to downstream processing
body:
Hi all,
I tend to delete bad tilts directly from the tomostar, delete the tiltstack directory, then regenerate the tiltstack directory. This works in my hands.
Best
Euan
On 11 Jun 2025, at 01:52, Richard Held <rgsheld@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿Are those tilts still listed in the tomostar file? If you are figuring out which tilts to exclude from the tiltstack, then going back to deselect them in the frameseries xml files, you may have to rerun ts_import and delete/remake your tilt-series xml files. 
On Saturday, June 7, 2025 at 1:39:15 PM UTC-7 dxh...@gmail.com wrote:
Hi Everyone,
Before I run tilt-series alignment, I always manually examine the tilt stack for bad tilts (i.e. ice coming into view, separate from Warp's metric-based selection. I list the frame series from which the bad tilts originate in a text file and ensure that the text file will point to the directories where the frameseries .xml files are listed. In deselected frameseries xml files, I can confirm that manual deselction has occurred. 
In this example frameseries xml file (TS_001_043_-50.00_20250329_090548_fractions.xml):
?
xml
 version
=
"1.0"
 encoding
=
"utf-8"
?>
<
Movie
 
DataDirectory
=
"20250329_frames"
 
UnselectFilter
=
"False"
 
UnselectManual
=
"True"
 
CTFResolutionEstimate
=
"11.4"
 
MeanFrameMovement
=
"0.0002054361"
 
MaskPercentage
=
"-1"
 
Bfactor
=
"0"
 
Weight
=
"1"
 
MagnificationCorrection
=
"1, 0, 0, 1"
>
Despite this deselection, I can see that the tiltseries xml file still selects that specific frameseries to contribute to the tiltseries.

>
This is a common issue across all my deselected frameseries. The issue isn't fixed even when I nuke the tiltseries directory and tomostar directories to rerun subsequent steps. I'm ok with manually editing the xml file to confirm the deselection for now, but has anyone also come across and fixed this issue?
Best,
Daniel
